tools.name: read_query
tools.description:
Dataverse SQL supports only basic SELECT queries with an explicit list of selected fields. 
If aggregates are used, do not mix aggregated and non-aggregated columns in the SELECT list without a GROUP BY clause. 
If a query requires comparing to an aggregate (for example, > AVG), do not use subqueries. 
Instead, split the logic into two queries: one to compute the aggregate and another to filter using literal values.
GETDATE() and GETUTCDATE() functions are supported in predicates but not in the SELECT list.
The following features are allowed:

TOP (without OFFSET). Example: SELECT TOP 20 col1 FROM table1
Simple column-to-literal, column-to-column and column-to-date value function WHERE filters. Example: SELECT age FROM table1 WHERE age > 10 and haircolor=eyecolor or hiredate<GETUTCDATE()
ORDER BY. Example: SELECT col1, age FROM table1 ORDER BY age
GROUP BY. Only the following aggregates are supported: COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX. Example: SELECT col1, COUNT(col2) AS player_count FROM table1 GROUP BY col1.
JOINs. Example: SELECT table1.col1, table1.col2, table2.col1 FROM table1 JOIN table2 ON table1.col1 = table2.col1

The following features are NOT supported:

Subqueries are not allowed in SELECT, WHERE, or FROM clauses.
HAVING. Example: SELECT col1,COUNT(id) AS player_count FROM table1 GROUP BY col1 HAVING COUNT(id) >= 3
DISTINCT in select lists. Example: SELECT DISTINCT col1 from table1
Subqueries (in SELECT, WHERE, FROM). Example: SELECT col1, col2 FROM table1 WHERE col1 IN (SELECT col3 FROM table2)
WITH keyword. Example: WITH Sales AS (WITH Sales SELECT CxId, SUM(Amt) AS Exp FROM Orders GROUP BY CxId ) SELECT CxId, Exp FROM Sales
OFFSET keyword. Example: SELECT Id, col1 FROM table1 ORDER BY Id OFFSET
UNION keyword. Example: SELECT col1, col2 FROM table1 WHERE col3 = 'Sales' UNION SELECT col1, col2 FROM table1 WHERE col3 = 'Marketing'
CAST keyword. Example: SELECT CAST('123' AS INT) AS ValueInt
CONVERT keyword. Example: SELECT CONVERT(DATE, OrderDate) AS OrderDateOnly, CONVERT(DECIMAL(10,2), Amt) AS Amount FROM Orders
CASE keyword. Example: SELECT Id, col1, col2, CASE WHEN Sal >= 100 THEN 'High' WHEN Sal >= 500 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS SalCat FROM table1
IF-ELSE keyword. Example: SELECT Id, col1, col2, CASE WHEN Sal > 100 THEN 'High' ELSE 'Low' END AS SalCat FROM table1
Advanced functions like: ROUND keyword. Example: SELECT PName, ROUND(Price, 2) AS RoundedPrice FROM table1
DATE functions. Example: DATEDIFF(), DATEADD(), DATE()
Complex conditions or unsupported aggregate argument types
JOINs on aggregates. Example: SELECT c.CxId, c.CxName, s.Exp FROM Cxs c JOIN (SELECT CxId, SUM(Amt) AS Exp FROM Orders GROUP BY CxId) AS s ON c.CxId = s.CxId WHERE s.Exp > 100

querytext.description: The SELECT SQL query to execute. Use list_tables tool to identify the logical table name. Then, use describe_table tool to find the logical names of the attributes along with their supported data types. Ensure that any SQL query you construct follows proper syntax and strictly adheres to the correct data types for each attribute.

tools.name: create_table
tools.description: Create a new table in Dataverse with the specified columns

tablename.description: Logical name (schema name) of the new Dataverse table. DO NOT include table prefixes, just the table name

displayname.description: Display name for the new table

item.description: Array of column definitions for the new table.

name.description: Display name of the column (no publisher prefix).

relatedtable.description: Required when type is 'lookup' or 'customer'. Logical name of the target table.

choices.description: Required when type is 'choice' or 'multiselect'. Array of option values.

label.description: Display label for the choice option.

value.description: Numeric value for the choice option.

tools.name: update_table
tools.description: Update an existing table in Dataverse by adding new columns

tablename.description: Logical name (schema name) of the existing table to update. Use list_tables to find the logical (schema) name of custom tables with default prefix like crb69_tablename.

item.description: Array of column definitions to add to the table.

name.description: Display name of the column (no publisher prefix).

relatedtable.description: Required when type is 'lookup' or 'customer'. Logical name of the target table.

choices.description: Required when type is 'choice' or 'multiselect'. Array of option values.

label.description: Display label for the choice option.

value.description: Numeric value for the choice option.

newDisplayName.description: New display name for the table. If not provided, the display name will not be changed.

description.description: Update Description for the table. If not provided, the description will not be changed.

tools.name: delete_table
tools.description: Proceed solely on explicit user consent then only DELETE a table in the Dataverse database

tablename.description: Logical name (schema name) of the table to delete. Use list_tables to find the logical (schema) name of custom tables with default prefix like crb69_tablename.

hasUserApproved.description: Set this to true only after asking the user if they are ok with the deletion and you have received an affirmative response

tools.name: create_record
tools.description: INSERT a row into a table in the Dataverse database and returns the GUID ID of the created record. Call describe first if the table's schema is unknown - DO NOT guess column logical names from display names. Choice columns require numeric option values, not labels.

tablename.description: The logical (schema) name of table to insert into. Use list_tables to find the logical name of custom tables with default prefix like crb69_tablename

item.description: Record field values as key-value pairs. Keys are column logical names from describe. Values: strings, numbers, or booleans for simple fields. For lookup/customer fields use an object: {"relatedTable": "account", "name": "Contoso Ltd", "recordId": "6D52BA41-0C1C-4B6F-91EF-731E78D3D34B"}. For choice fields use the numeric option value. For multi-select choice use comma-separated values like "100000002,100000004".

tools.name: update_record
tools.description: UPDATE a row into a table in the Dataverse database. Call describe first if the table's schema is unknown - DO NOT guess column logical names from display names. Choice columns require numeric option values, not labels.

tablename.description: The logical name (schema name) of the table to update a record in. Use list_tables to find the logical name of custom tables with default prefix like crb69_tablename.

recordId.description: The GUID of the record id

item.description: Properties to update as key-value pairs. Keys are column logical names from describe. Values: strings, numbers, or booleans for simple fields. For lookup/customer fields use an object: {"relatedTable": "account", "name": "Contoso Ltd", "recordId": "6D52BA41-0C1C-4B6F-91EF-731E78D3D34B"}. For choice fields use the numeric option value. For multi-select choice use comma-separated values like "100000002,100000004".

tools.name: delete_record
tools.description: Proceed solely on explicit user consent then only DELETE a row from a table in the Dataverse database

tablename.description: The logical name (schema name) of the table to delete a record from. Use list_tables to find the logical (schema) name of custom tables with default prefix like crb69_tablename.

hasUserApproved.description: Set this to true only after asking the user if they are ok with the deltion and you have received an affirmative response

recordId.description: The GUID of the record id

tools.name: search
tools.description:
Find table schemas, skills, and scopes in Dataverse by keyword.

Searches across:
- Table schemas (when you need table structure/fields)
- Skills (business process instructions)
- Scopes (scopes with their entity lists)

Returns filesystem-style paths:
  tables/{tablename}
  skills/{skillname}
  scopes/{scopeName}

Optional: Pass scope to filter table results to a specific scope's entities.

Examples:
  search("opportunity")
  → Find relevant skills, table schemas, and scopes for opportunity

  search("sales", scope="msft_dvtablesearch_aiplugin_model_SalesHub")
  → Find tables within Sales scope matching 'sales'

Use describe() tool on returned paths to see full details.

scope.description: Optional. Scope table results to a specific scope's entities. Get scope names from describe('scopes/').

tools.name: upsert_skill
tools.description: Add or update a Business Skill in Dataverse. To update an existing skill, provide id or name to identify it. Provide unlinkScope: true when updating scope to replace existing scopes rather than merge.

body.description: The detailed body content of the skill

id.description: The GUID of the skill to update. Preferred over name when available to avoid ambiguity.

uniquename.description: Unique name for the skill. Auto-generated if not provided. Must start with a customization prefix(ex: new_).

scope.description: JSON array of app scope names to associate with the skill, e.g. ["scopeA", "scopeB"]

unlinkScope.description: When true, replaces existing scopes with the provided scope list. When false or omitted, merges with existing scopes. Required when scope is provided.

tools.name: create_skill_resource
tools.description: Creates a skillresource record linked to a skill. Once the record exists, upload the file content using init_file_upload and commit_file_upload. Upload values: tablename: "skillresource", recordId: {skillresourceid from result}, fileAttributeName: "filecontent", fileName: {just the file name, not the full path}.

skillid.description: The GUID of the skill that owns this resource

filename.description: Relative path of the file from the skill root (where SKILL.md resides). Example: path/to/resourcefile.md

uniquename.description: Unique name for the resource. Auto-generated from the skill uniquename and filename if not provided. Must start with a customization prefix(ex: new_).

tools.name: delete_skill
tools.description: Delete a Business Skill by name or ID. Prefer id over name to avoid ambiguity; name will fail if multiple skills share the same name.

name.description: The name of the skill to delete

id.description: The GUID of the skill to delete

tools.name: describe
tools.description:
Use this tool to get comprehensive details for any path from search().

			Works with all resource types:
			- Catalog listing → List all tables, skills, or scopes
			- Data records → Full JSON with URL
			- Table schemas → Markdown with fields, types, relationships, example queries
			- Skills → Full skill with description and body/instructions, and list of resources
			- Skill resources → resource files attached to a skill (content truncated for large files)
			- Scopes → Scope metadata with name, description, and entity list

			Path formats (from search results):
			  tables/                                           - List all available tables
			  tables/account/records/{uuid}                    - Full record of {uuid} in account table
			  tables/opportunity                                - Opportunity table schema
			  skills/                                          - List all available business skills
			  skills/{skillname}                               - Business Skill detail and resource list
			  skills/{skillname}/skillresources/{filename}     - Business Skill resource file content by filename relative path
			  skills/{skillname}/skillresources/{uuid}         - Business Skill resource using skillresource ID
			  scopes/                                          - List all available scopes
			  scopes/{scopeName}                               - Scope details with entity list

			Optional: Pass scope to filter tables/ listing to a specific scope's entities.

			Examples:
			  describe('tables/')
			  → List all available tables

			  describe('tables/', scope='msft_dvtablesearch_aiplugin_model_SalesHub')
			  → List only tables belonging to the Sales scope

			  describe('tables/account/records/{guid}')
			  → Complete account data with URL

			  describe('tables/opportunity')
			  → Full schema with SQL field types, relationships, example queries

			  describe('skills/Sales-Call-Logging')
			  → Full business skill with description and body/instructions, and list of resources

			  describe('skills/Sales-Call-Logging/skillresources/path/to/resourcefile.md')
			  → Skill resource file content, which is truncated if too large. Use the file_download tool to download the full file with tablename:skillresource, recordId:{skillresourceid}, fileAttributeName:'filecontent'

			  describe('skills/Sales-Call-Logging/skillresources/{uuid}')
			  → Skill resource using skillresource ID

			  describe('scopes/')
			  → List all available scopes

			  describe('scopes/msft_dvtablesearch_aiplugin_model_SalesHub')
			  → Scope details with entities

path.description: Path from search() results (e.g., tables/, tables/account/records/{guid}, tables/opportunity, skills/, skills/{name}, skills/{name}/skillresources/{path/to/file}, skills/{name}/skillresources/{id}, scopes/, scopes/{scopeName})

scope.description: Optional. Scope tables/ or skills/ listing to a specific scope's entities. Get scope names from describe('scopes/').

tools.name: search_data
tools.description:
Search for data and within files in Dataverse for a given scope. This tool searches for records which contain query keywords. It also searches within file contents for Dataverse tables that have file columns enabled for search, and returns matching content.

			Returns filesystem-style paths for matching records (e.g. tables/account/records/{guid}).
			If the search was within a file content it would also include the matching content. 
				e.g. 
					tables/account/records/{guid}
					content: ...text from the file that matched the search query...

			Required: Pass scope to limit the search to correct scopes. Call describe('scopes/') and describe('scopes/{scopeName}') to find the appropriate scope.

			Examples:
			  search_data('contoso', scope='msft_dvtablesearch_aiplugin_model_SalesHub')
			  → Find records and matching file content for 'contoso' within the Sales scope
			Use describe() tool on returned paths to see full record details.

			If you need to find the right table schema or skill, use the search() tool instead.

scope.description: Search scope. Call describe('scopes/') and scopes/{scopeName} to find the appropriate scope

tools.name: init_file_upload
tools.description:
Step 1 of file upload: generates a SAS URL for uploading a file to a Dataverse record. Returns a SAS URL and continuationToken. Upload the file content via HTTP PUT to the SAS URL, then call commit_file_upload with the continuationToken. Important: Do not change the file extension if it fails to upload.

			Examples:
			  Upload a skill resource file:
			    tablename: "skillresource", recordId: {skillresourceid}, fileAttributeName: "filecontent", fileName: {resourcefile.md}

tablename.description: Logical name of the table containing the record

recordId.description: GUID of the record to attach the file to

fileAttributeName.description: Logical name of the file column on the table

fileName.description: Name of the file to upload including extension

tools.name: commit_file_upload
tools.description: Step 3 of file upload: commits a completed file upload. Call this only after the file has been successfully uploaded via HTTP PUT to the SAS URL from init_file_upload.

fileName.description: Name of the uploaded file including extension

tools.name: file_download
tools.description:
Generates a SAS URL for downloading a file from a Dataverse record.

			Examples:
			  Download a skill resource file:
			    tablename: "skillresource", recordId: {skillresourceid}, fileAttributeName: "filecontent"

tablename.description: Logical name of the table containing the record

fileAttributeName.description: Logical name of the file column on the table
